qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default Credentials Vulnerability - Root Account with Weak Password
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-1188: Initialization with Hard-Coded Network Configuration Credentials
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns hardcoded default root credentials created during initialization and exploited through the login endpoint, not the advisory's unescaped SQL LIKE wildcard handling in model/token.go or /api/token/search.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: unescaped user-controlled wildcard characters in SQL LIKE clauses in model/token.go SearchUserTokens, reachable through /api/token/search and causing token-search denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-w6x6-9fp7-fqm4 identifies github.com/QuantumNous/new-api model/token.go:70, where keyword and token parameters are concatenated into LIKE patterns without escaping %, _, or backslash.
default_credentials_vulnerability identifies model/main.go createRootAccountIfNeed and POST /api/user/login default root:123456 authentication, not token search or SQL LIKE wildcard injection.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so no PoC execution was applicable.
